sections.title: v0.5.0
sections.description:
**Release Date:** February 2026
**Codename:** Platform Hardening
**Previous Version:** v0.3.0

items.name: Executive Summary
items.description: v0.5.0 is a comprehensive platform hardening release that transforms OpenAgentic from a prototype into a production-grade enterprise AI platform. This release addresses critical security gaps, adds architectural resilience, replaces the Flowise workflow engine with native Agentic

items.name: Breaking Changes
items.description: ### Flowise Removed The Flowise workflow engine has been completely removed from the platform. All workflow functionality is now handled by the native **OpenAgentic Flows** engine.

items.name: New Features
items.description: ### 1. OpenAgentic Flows — Native Workflow Engine  A fully native visual workflow builder replacing Flowise, with execution engine and 21 node types across 7 categories.

items.name: Infrastructure & Operations
items.description: ### Multi-Environment Deployment  | Environment | URL | Cluster | Pods |

items.description: ### New Endpoints | Endpoint | Method | Description | |----------|--------|-------------|

items.name: Database Changes
items.description: ### New Models | Model | Table | Purpose | |-------|-------|---------|

items.name: Known Issues
items.description: 1. **PostgreSQL bottleneck**: Single primary shared by all API pods. Under high HPA scaling (10 pods), connection pooling may be needed. 2. **ARM64 QEMU builds**: Multi-arch builds take 5-7 min due to ARM64 emulation. Native ARM64 builder would improve this. 3. **Milvus cold star

sections.title: v0.6.6
sections.description:
**Release date:** 2026-04-18.
**Theme:** Close every HITL escape hatch; ship the platform with no un-reviewable security bypasses.

items.name: Breaking changes
items.description: - **`DISABLE_HITL_GATE` environment variable is a no-op.** The backdoor that let HIGH-risk tool calls (azure_delete_*, aws_terminate_*, etc.) auto-approve when the env var was set has been deleted from `ToolApprovalGate.evaluate()`. Dev/CI auto-approval now has to go through the

items.name: What shipped, by UC blocker
items.description: ### UC-A14 — HITL gate for destructive Azure delete - `ToolApprovalGate`: backdoor env-var check removed. `DEFAULT_HIGH_RISK_PATTERNS` widened to cover `terminate|stop|shutdown|deallocate` for AWS/GCP/Azure/K8s tool families. - `submitApproval()` now writes an `approval_decision`

items.name: P5 — FedRAMP/HIPAA tenant isolation
items.description: - `docs/ac/data-isolation-p0.md` extended with a v0.6.6 P5 Expansion section covering the threat model, the data-class inventory (~20 tables + Milvus + MinIO), and acceptance criteria. - Prisma migration `20260418_rls_expansion`:   - Enables Postgres RLS + FORCE on `user_memory`,

items.name: P6 — AWS MCP parity
items.description: `services/mcps/oap-aws-mcp/server.py`: 8 → 31 typed tools. All read-only; destructive ops tracked for 0.6.7. New tools delegate to `_execute_aws_command` for OBO consistency.  | Family | Tools |

items.name: P7 — GCP MCP parity
items.description: `services/mcps/oap-gcp-mcp/src/server.py`: 28 → 46 typed tools. All read-only.  | Family | Tools |

items.name: P8 — Background-job state machine
items.description: - Prisma migration `20260418_background_job_state_machine`: adds `checkpoint_data JSONB`, `resume_at TIMESTAMP(3)`, `state_transitions JSONB` to `background_jobs`, plus a partial index on `(status, resume_at) WHERE resume_at IS NOT NULL` for the poller query. - `BackgroundJobStat

items.name: P9 — Admin Test Harness wiring
items.description: Already landed pre-release (`TestHarnessView` lazy import + sidebar entry in `AdminPortal.tsx` + `switch`-case). Verified this release: Monitoring & Logs → Test Harness is reachable.

items.name: Infrastructure
items.description: - **All openagentic pods on amd64** (hal/dalek). Fixed Bitnami Redis + Postgres subchart values to set `kubernetes.io/arch: amd64` nodeSelectors. ARM Pi nodes were causing RDB/AOF format crashloops on pod reschedule and cross-node CNI hangs to hal. - **SonarQube plumbing**:   - `

items.name: Test counts
items.description: | Suite | Cases | Status | |---|---|---| | ToolApprovalGate | 8 | pass |

items.name: Live verification evidence
items.description: - `docs/releases/0.6.6-evidence/uc/UC-A13/` — DLP pre-LLM block on AWS key (NDJSON + assertions). - `docs/releases/0.6.6-evidence/uc/UC-A14/` — HITL gate on destructive Azure delete (NDJSON + assertions). - `docs/releases/0.6.6-evidence/uc/UC-A17/` — synth_execute exposed + sandb

items.name: Known gaps / tracked for 0.6.7
items.description: - BackgroundJobPoller interval worker + `ctx.checkpoint` agent-runtime API + ParkedJobs UI sidebar. - Destructive AWS/GCP MCP tool wrappers (`aws_delete_*`, `aws_terminate_*`, `gcp_delete_*`, `gcp_stop_*`) — will auto-route through ToolApprovalGate HIGH via the widened pattern. -

items.name: Theme
items.description: v0.6.7 was scoped to complete the SSE → NDJSON migration, adopt the Anthropic Messages canonical stream event union, ship the Phase F/G/H/I inline event families, rewrite the cloud MCP auth flows as direct-passthrough / OIDC federation, and bring the chat UX to Claude.ai / Gemini

items.name: What actually shipped
items.description: ### Streaming wire (✅ shipped)  | Phase | Commits | Landed |

items.name: What's BROKEN / MISSING — reasons NOT to ship
items.description: ### 🔴 Critical — block release  1. **Chat UX visual parity** (task #166, in progress). Live chat-dev.openagentic.io does NOT visually match `docs/release-plans/v0.6.7-ux-mockups/01-cloud-ops.html`. The shipped components have the correct behavior but pre-mockup className strings

items.name: Contract matrix snapshot
items.description: ``` Pre-v0.6.7: ~50 event types Post-v0.6.7: 150 shipped + 3 pending + 19 dead (emit sites w/o UI consumer) = 172 total

items.name: Breaking changes
items.description: - `DISABLE_HITL_GATE` is still a no-op (from v0.6.6). - `ENABLE_AUTH` env var on mcp-proxy is now a no-op (gate removed in source; helm values still set it for compatibility). - `NormalizedStreamEvent` → `AgenticStreamEvent` (old name preserved as `@deprecated` alias for one rele

items.name: Upgrade path from v0.6.6
items.description: 1. Helm upgrade with `values-k3s-local.yaml` + `values-local-registry.yaml` (chart version 0.6.4+ still covers v0.6.7 services). 2. First boot re-ingests all docs (~30-60s — see bug #165). 3. Verify AAD login still works (AAD app reg client ID `71f4ffd5-…` unchanged).

items.name: Decision
items.description: **Do not ship until at minimum:** - [ ] Chat UX visually matches mockup 01-cloud-ops.html (task #166) - [ ] Codemode renders in the new UX (not yet verified)

items.name: Summary
items.description: - **Enterprise chatmode (Claude-Code-grade)** — single `chatmode` pipeline, 12 T1 primitives, per-T1 description builders, full SDK canonical events. - **Glob-based permissions UI** — Claude-Code allow/deny/ask rules replace the legacy regex-tier ToolApprovalGate. - **Inline tool

items.name: What's new
items.description: ### Enterprise chatmode (Claude-Code-grade)  Single `chatmode` pipeline. 12 T1 primitives — `tool_search`, `agent_search`, `Task`, `agent_send`, `agent_list`, `agent_stop`, `read_large_result`, `web_search`, `web_fetch`, `synth`, `pattern_save`, `pattern_recall`. Per-T1 descripti

items.name: Breaking changes
items.description: - The legacy regex-tier ToolApprovalGate is deprecated in favor of the glob-based permission rules at `/admin#tool-permissions`. Existing approval policies are migrated forward on boot; admins should review the new rule set after upgrade. - `chatmode` is now the only chat pipelin
